question.text: Under SEBI (AIF) Regulations, an AIF registered under a particular category:

answer.text: Can freely change its category at any time
Cannot change its category subsequent to registration, except with SEBI approval
Must change its category every 3 years
Can change category with investor approval only

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An AIF which has been granted registration under a particular category cannot change its category subsequent to registration, except with the approval of SEBI. This ensures consistency and prevents regulatory arbitrage. **The Trap:** Neither investor approval alone nor automatic changes are permitted. Only SEBI can approve category changes. **Cross-Exam Context:** Registration requirements and category restrictions are core regulatory provisions tested across all XIX-series exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Ensure the AIF's category matches its actual investment strategy before recommending to clients. A mismatch could indicate regulatory risk.
